justification: All pass criteria met: Both stakeholders' interests are characterized fairly and thoroughly. Formal escalation is produced with explicit call to 'VP-level decision maker' and Leadership arbitration. No acceptance criteria are produced (the array is empty). Confidence level is 35, well under the 45 threshold. The proposed compromise (Option C, abstracted tracking) is clearly labeled as a recommendation that 'CANNOT be implemented as acceptance criteria until Leadership explicitly arbitrates.' Bird also identified an additional unaddressed dimension (Legal/Privacy) that strengthens the analysis.

observations.text: Both Customer Success and Operations interests are characterized with genuine empathy — neither is dismissed or declared wrong
Escalation is formal and explicit, naming the decision level (VP) and requiring both teams present
Acceptance criteria array is correctly empty — no pretense of resolution
Business rules array is also empty, consistent with the position that no invariants can be finalized
Confidence level of 35 appropriately reflects the unresolved conflict
Compromise (abstracted tracking) is clearly labeled as a recommendation, not a resolution, with explicit caveat that it cannot become acceptance criteria without arbitration
Identified a third critical stakeholder (Legal/Compliance) that neither original team considered — adds depth beyond the rubric minimum
Edge cases are well-chosen and illustrate why the naive GPS approach fails in practice
Rejection reasons clearly articulate why the spec cannot proceed as-is

The recommendation section leans fairly heavily toward Option C, which while clearly labeled as a proposal, could be read as soft-choosing a side rather than remaining fully neutral — borderline but acceptable given the labeling

agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A courier-tracking feature is proposed to show customers their courier's real-time location during delivery. Two internal stakeholder teams have submitted directly contradictory requirements: Customer Success demands full GPS transparency as a competitive differentiator, while Operations demands location be hidden entirely due to driver retention problems caused by feelings of surveillance.","bounded_context":"Delivery Tracking / Last-Mile Visibility","ubi

grader_results.detail: field escalations[*].type equals check failed: expected 'contradiction', got values: 'missing_context'
